questions.text: Statements: Good voice is a natural gift but one has to keep practicing to improve and excel well in the field of music.Conclusions:I. Natural gifts need nurturing and care.II. Even though your voice is not good, one can keep practicing.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This is correct. The statement provides a specific example (a good voice) of a 'natural gift' that requires 'practicing' (nurturing) to 'improve and excel'. Conclusion I is a logical generalization of this idea. Conclusion II is not supported as the statement does not discuss what one should do if their voice is not good.

This is incorrect. The statement's scope is limited to people with a 'good voice'. It offers no information or advice for those whose voice is not good, so Conclusion II cannot be logically derived from the given text.

This option is incorrect. For an 'either/or' choice to be considered, both conclusions usually need to be plausible under different interpretations. Here, Conclusion I clearly follows, while Conclusion II clearly does not, making this option invalid.
This is incorrect because Conclusion I is a valid inference. The statement explicitly links a 'natural gift' (good voice) with the need for practice, which is a form of 'nurturing and care'.

questions.explanation: The statement directly links a natural gift (good voice) with the need for practice (nurturing) to excel. This strongly supports Conclusion I. The statement only discusses what to do with a "good voice" and does not provide any information about what one should do if their voice is not good.
hints: natural gift but one has to keep practicing

questions.text: Statements: The Government of country X has recently announced several concessions and offered attractive package tours for foreign visitors.Conclusions:I. Now, more number of foreign tourists will visit the country.II. The Government of country X seems to be serious in attracting tourists.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I suggests that more tourists will visit due to the concessions. However, there is no guarantee that such offers will necessarily result in increased tourism.
Conclusion II is supported by the government's actions, which demonstrate an intent to attract tourists. However, conclusion I also logically follows, making this option incorrect.
This option suggests that only one of the conclusions can follow, but both conclusions logically follow from the statement.

The government's concessions and attractive packages demonstrate a serious intent to attract tourists (conclusion II), and it is plausible to conclude that such efforts could result in more tourists visiting (conclusion I).

questions.explanation: The government's actions (announcing concessions and packages) are clear evidence of a serious effort to attract tourists (Conclusion II). It is a logical assumption that such actions are taken with the expectation that they will be effective, thus making it likely that more tourists will visit (Conclusion I).

questions.text: Statements: Parents are prepared to pay any price for an elite education to their children.Conclusions:I. All parents these days are very well off.II. Parents have an obsessive passion for a perfect development of their children through good schooling.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I assumes that all parents are well off, which is not directly supported by the statement. The statement focuses on the willingness to pay, not the financial status of all parents.

Conclusion II is correct because the statement indicates that parents are willing to pay a high price for elite education, which suggests a strong desire for their children's perfect development through good schooling.
The statement does not provide a basis for choosing between I or II, as only II logically follows from parents willingness to pay for elite education.
Conclusion II does follow based on the given statement, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The willingness to pay "any price" points to a strong desire or "obsessive passion" for what they perceive as the best for their children (Conclusion II). The statement does not provide information about the financial status of all parents, only their willingness to pay, so Conclusion I is an overgeneralization.

questions.text: Statements: Quality has a price tag. Pakistan is allocating lots of funds to education.Conclusions:I) Quality of education in Pakistan will improve soon.II) Funding alone can enhance the quality of education.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: According to the statement, funding is necessary to improve quality and Pakistan is allocating funds to education. This means that quality of education will improve in Pakistan. So, I follows.But funding alone is sufficient to enhance quality, is not true. So, II does not follow.

According to the statement, funding is necessary to improve quality and Pakistan is allocating funds to education. This means that quality of education will improve in Pakistan. So, I follows.But funding alone is sufficient to enhance quality, is not true. So, II does not follow.

According to the statement, funding is necessary to improve quality and Pakistan is allocating funds to education. This means that quality of education will improve in Pakistan. So, I follows.But funding alone is sufficient to enhance quality, is not true. So, II does not follow.

According to the statement, funding is necessary to improve quality and Pakistan is allocating funds to education. This means that quality of education will improve in Pakistan. So, I follows.But funding alone is sufficient to enhance quality, is not true. So, II does not follow.

According to the statement, funding is necessary to improve quality and Pakistan is allocating funds to education. This means that quality of education will improve in Pakistan. So, I follows.But funding alone is sufficient to enhance quality, is not true. So, II does not follow.

questions.explanation: According to the statement, funding is necessary to improve quality and Pakistan is allocating funds to education. This means that quality of education will improve in Pakistan. So, I follows.But funding alone is sufficient to enhance quality, is not true. So, II does not follow.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.Though the State cultivates only 3.2 lakh tons of mangoes. they are of premium quality and with mangoes becoming second most consumed fruit in the world after grapes, the government has been trying exporting it through sea route which is cheaper. An experiment which was done in this regard last year hasred successful.Most of the other exports are though sea routes which is cheaper.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The passage does not provide absolute certainty that the government will continue using the sea route for mango exports, as it only mentions an experiment that was successful. Hence, this option cannot be definitely true.

The passage suggests that the success of the experiment and the common use of sea routes for other exports indicate a high likelihood that mango exports will continue via the sea route. This makes the statement probably true.
While the passage does not provide explicit confirmation of future plans, it gives enough context (successful experiment and cost-effectiveness) to infer a probable continuation of sea route exports.
The passage provides positive indicators (successful experiment and cost-effectiveness) that suggest the continuation of sea route exports, making this option unlikely.
The passage's successful experimentation and mention of the sea route's advantages make it unreasonable to conclude that the statement is definitely false.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Probably true' because the passage provides evidence supporting the likelihood of continued mango exports via the sea route. The experiment was successful, and the sea route is noted as cheaper, which aligns with other exports. This context supports a probable inference even if not explicitly stated. Other options are incorrect because they either overstate certainty or do not adequately consider the implications of the provided data.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.A tiger, when killing its natural prey, which it does either by stalking or lying-in wait for it, depends for the success of its attack on its speed and, to a lesser extent, on the condition of its teeth and claws. When, therefore, a tiger is suffering from one or more painful wounds or when its teeth are missing or defective and its claws worn down, and it is unable to catch animals it has been accustomed to eating, it is driven by the necessity to killing human beings.Human beings are the natural prey of tigers.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Definitely true: The text explicitly states that tigers kill their natural prey by stalking or lying in wait. It then goes on to explain that tigers only attack humans when they are injured or unable to hunt their normal prey. This directly contradicts the idea that humans are their natural prey.
Probably true: There is no information in the text to suggest that humans are likely to be the natural prey of tigers. The context points strongly to the opposite.

Inadequate data: The passage provides specific reasons why tigers might attack humans, and these reasons are presented as exceptions to their normal hunting behavior. This is sufficient data to refute the idea that humans are natural prey.

Probably false: While "probably false" is closer to the correct answer, the text offers a clear explanation that contradicts the statement, making it definitively false rather than just likely to be false.
"Human beings are the natural prey of tigers" is definitely false.

questions.explanation: "Human beings are the natural prey of tigers" is definitely false.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.A radical new surgery procedure, laughed at not long ago, is holding out fresh hope for patients of cardiac myopathy, or enlargement of the heart. The technique, now in Pakistan, allows patients to go home two weeks after the Operation, to lead a near-normal sedentary life. Cardiac myopathy is a condition that has a variety of causative factors. An attack from one of the 20 identified viruses, parasite infection, long-term alcohol abuse and blood pressure could bring it on, and in rare cases, it could follow child birth and is even known to run in families. The condition is marked by an increase in the size of the heart's chambers and a decrease in the efficiency of pumping.Cardiac myopathy is hereditary.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Although the passage suggests a genetic component by stating it can 'run in families,' it does not provide conclusive evidence that cardiac myopathy is definitely hereditary.
The passage suggests that cardiac myopathy can 'run in families,' indicating a potential hereditary component, but it is not conclusive. Therefore, 'probably true' is the most accurate choice.

While the passage provides some information about familial patterns, it does not provide enough data to definitively determine the hereditary nature, making this option plausible, but 'probably true' is more appropriate given the context.
The passage provides some indication of a hereditary component in cardiac myopathy by mentioning it can 'run in families,' making this option unlikely.
The passage explicitly mentions that cardiac myopathy can 'run in families,' which contradicts the idea that it is definitely not hereditary.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Probably true' because the passage states that cardiac myopathy is 'even known to run in families,' suggesting a potential hereditary link. However, it does not provide definitive proof, which is why 'probably true' is more fitting than 'definitely true.' Other options such as 'Data inadequate' and 'Probably false' are less appropriate because the passage does provide some evidence of a hereditary component. 'Definitely false' is incorrect as it contradicts the information given in the passage.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.Primary education in Sindh is in a poor shape. Pupils in over 50 percent of the schools read in the open throughout the year. Over six million children born to lower Income group parents remain unlettered. Not even one percent of the 63,000 primary schools have facilities of furniture, toilet, drinking water and games. 3 113 new teachers have been appointed in the current financial year out of which 2747 are women. Now each of the 13,270 primary schools have at least two teachers.630 primary schools in Sindh have all the facilities like furniture, toilet, drinking water, games.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The statement cannot be definitely true because the passage clearly indicates that significantly fewer than 630 schools have all the facilities, making this option incorrect.
Given the specific statistics provided, the statement is unlikely to be true, as the number of schools with facilities is a very small fraction of the total, making this option incorrect.
The passage provides explicit data about the number of schools with facilities, so this option is incorrect due to sufficient data being available.
Although the passage suggests a lack of facilities in the majority of schools, the statement is not just probably false but definitely false, as the specific data disproves it.
The passage clearly states that less than one percent of the 63,000 primary schools have all the facilities, meaning the statement that 630 schools have them is definitely false.

questions.explanation: The statement '630 primary schools in Sindh have all the facilities like furniture, toilet, drinking water, games' is definitely false. The passage states that less than one percent of 63,000 schools are equipped with these facilities, which translates to significantly fewer than 630 schools. Therefore, the statement directly contradicts the passage. The options suggesting it could be true or that data is inadequate are incorrect since the passage provides clear and sufficient data to reach a definitive conclusion.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.The Government of Pakistan has urged to Provincial Governments to create more jobs opportunities for women. All-out efforts are to be made to have more training facilities for women so that they can be gainfully employed. It is in pursuance of the recent policy decisions taken by the Government to make women self-reliant. The Provincials have been asked to end discrimination against the fair sex so far new jobs are concerned. The wage for women workers should also the strictly adhered to. it has been emphasized.Women should he given higher wages than men to make them self-reliant.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option suggests that women should receive higher wages than men, which contradicts the passage's emphasis on fairness and equality. It is not about giving preferential treatment based on gender but ensuring equal opportunity and pay.

This option implies a likelihood that women should receive higher wages than men, which is not supported by the passage. The passage focuses on eliminating discrimination and ensuring fairness, not on providing unequal pay.

The passage provides sufficient data emphasizing equality in job opportunities and wages. Therefore, the data is not inadequate; the statement about higher wages for women is contrary to the passage's principles.

While this option leans towards the correct interpretation, it still leaves room for uncertainty. The passage clearly advocates for equal treatment, making the statement about higher wages for women definitely false.
The statement is definitely false as it suggests inequality in wages based on gender, which is against the passage's call for fairness and adherence to equal wage policies for women.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Definitely false. The passage emphasizes the government's efforts to eliminate discrimination and ensure fairness in job opportunities and wages for women. Suggesting that women should receive higher wages than men contradicts this message of equality and fairness. All other options incorrectly assume a level of truth to the statement or suggest data inadequacy, which is not the case, as the passage clearly outlines the principles of equal treatment and adherence to fair wage policies.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.The water resources of our country are very much underutilized. The main reason of this underutilization is the lack of capital and technology. A large portion of our water resources is wasted due to floods and unwise use of water for irrigation as well as domestic purposes. We can make full use of our water resources by building dams on rivers and by adopting policy of awareness among people not to waste water.The country does not have enough funds to utilize such water resources.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The passage explicitly states that lack of capital is a main reason for underutilizing water resources, strongly supporting the inference.
While the passage indicates the lack of funds as a reason, it is definitive rather than probable, making this option less accurate.
The passage provides sufficient information about the lack of capital affecting water resource development, making this option incorrect.
The passage directly contradicts this option by stating that lack of capital is a main issue, so this option is incorrect.
The passage clearly mentions lack of capital as a main reason, ruling out the possibility of this statement being false.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Definitely true'. The passage clearly states that the primary cause for underutilization of water resources is the lack of capital and technology. This directly supports the statement that the country does not have enough funds to develop its water resources. Other options are incorrect because they either misinterpret the definitiveness of the passage's information or contradict the explicit statements made within it.

The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. options.explanation: The passage explicitly states that lack of capital is a main reason for underutilizing water resources, strongly supporting the inference.

The passage provides sufficient information about the lack of capital affecting water resource development, making this option incorrect.
The passage directly contradicts this option by stating that lack of capital is a main issue, so this option is incorrect.
The passage clearly mentions lack of capital as a main reason, ruling out the possibility of this statement being false.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Definitely true'. The passage clearly states that the primary cause for underutilization of water resources is the lack of capital and technology. This directly supports the statement that the country does not have enough funds to develop its water resources. Other options are incorrect because they either misinterpret the definitiveness of the passage's information or contradict the explicit statements made within it.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.Urban services have not expanded fast enough to cope with urban expansion. Low investment allocations have tended to be underspent. Both public (e.g. water and sewage) and private (e.g. low-income area housing) infrastructure quality has declined. This impact of the environment in which children live and the supporting services available to them when they fall ill, seems clear. The decline in average food availability and the rise in absolute poverty point in the same unsatisfactory direction.There is nothing to boast about urban services.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The passage highlights numerous deficiencies in urban services, including inadequate expansion, underinvestment, and declining quality. These factors support the conclusion that there is indeed 'nothing to boast about' regarding urban services, making this inference definitely true.

While the negative aspects of urban services are evident in the passage, the conclusion 'nothing to boast about' is strongly suggested rather than merely probable. Therefore, this option is not the best choice.

The passage provides sufficient evidence of problems with urban services, including specifics about investment shortfalls and declining quality. Thus, the data is adequate to support the negative assessment.
The passage does not offer any positive information about urban services. The evidence presented strongly supports the negative inference rather than suggesting it might be false.
The passage strongly indicates deficiencies in urban services, with no evidence to contradict the negative assessment. Therefore, the inference cannot be definitely false.

questions.explanation: The passage presents a series of negative statements regarding urban services, including underinvestment, insufficient expansion, and declining infrastructure quality. These points collectively lead to a conclusion that there is 'nothing to boast about' in terms of urban services. This inference is definitely true, as it accurately reflects the overall negative assessment conveyed by the passage. Other options, such as 'probably true' or 'data inadequate,' fail to capture the definitive nature of the conclusion drawn from the passage.

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Low investment allocations have tended to be underspent. Both public (e.g. water and sewage) and private (e.g. low-income area housing) infrastructure quality has declined. This impact of the environment in which children live and the supporting services available to them when they fall ill, seems clear. The decline in average food availability and the rise in absolute poverty point in the same unsatisfactory direction.The environment around plays an important role on the health status.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The passage clearly states that "Urban services have not expanded fast enough to cope with urban expansion." Additionally, the passage provides supporting details about insufficient investment and declining infrastructure quality, which directly support the inference.

While the passage provides strong evidence, the use of 'probably' implies some uncertainty that isn't supported by the explicit statements in the passage. The passage is definitive about the lack of expansion.
The passage provides detailed information about the lack of urban services expansion, investment issues, and infrastructure decline. Therefore, the data is adequate to support the inference.
The passage explicitly contradicts this by stating the failure of urban services to keep pace with expansion, so this option does not align with the passage's content.
The passage provides explicit evidence that urban services have not expanded as needed, making this option directly contradicted by the passage.

questions.explanation: The passage explicitly states that urban services are lagging behind urban expansion, supported by facts about investment and infrastructure quality. Thus, the inference that the environment plays an important role in health status is 'Definitely true.' The other options fail to align with the explicit and clear statements in the passage, which provide a definitive account of the situation.

Low investment allocations have tended to be underspent. Both public (e.g. water and sewage) and private (e.g. low-income area housing) infrastructure quality has declined. This impact of the environment in which children live and the supporting services available to them when they fall ill, seems clear. The decline in average food availability and the rise in absolute poverty point in the same unsatisfactory direction.Through adequate provisions of funds were made but they remained unspent.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The passage does not support this inference as it states that adequate provisions of funds were made but remained unspent, contradicting the notion that low investment allocations were underspent.
This inference is not supported by the passage. The statement about unspent funds contradicts the idea that low investment allocations were underspent.
The passage provides sufficient information to determine the truthfulness of the inference. Thus, the data is not inadequate.
The passage gives a clear contradiction to the inference, making it definitely false rather than probably false.

The passage clearly states, 'adequate provisions of funds were made but they remained unspent,' which directly contradicts the inference that 'low investment allocations have tended to be underspent.' Therefore, this inference is definitely false.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Definitely false.' The passage explicitly states that 'adequate provisions of funds were made but they remained unspent,' which directly contradicts the inference that 'low investment allocations have tended to be underspent.' This makes the inference definitely false. Other options are incorrect because they either misinterpret the passage, suggest insufficient data, or fail to recognize the clear contradiction presented in the passage.

questions.text: Statements:Pakistan is a multilingual country. Urdu is the national language of Pakistan.Conclusions:I. AII Pakistanis should learn many languages. II. To be a Pakistani one needs to learn Urdu.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This is incorrect. The statement that Pakistan is multilingual describes the country as a whole, not a requirement for its individual citizens. The statement does not impose an obligation on all Pakistanis to learn multiple languages.

This is incorrect. While Urdu is the national language, the statement does not establish learning it as a condition for being a Pakistani. It describes the status of the language, not a requirement for the people.

options.text: Neither Conclusion I nor Conclusion II is followed
options.explanation: This is correct. The given statements are factual and descriptive. They do not contain any information that would support the prescriptive conclusions about what Pakistanis 'should' or 'need to' do.

options.text: Both Conclusion I and II are followed
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Since neither conclusion can be logically derived from the factual statements, it's impossible for both to follow.

questions.explanation: The statements are factual descriptions and do not contain any prescription or requirement. They do not imply what people should or need to do. Therefore, neither conclusion can be logically drawn from the statements provided.

questions.text: Artificial Language:What will be the meaning of 'walkaway'? If mignelasan means cupboard Lasanpoen means boardwalk Cuopdansa means Pullman
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: 'Lasan' is common to 'Lasanpoen' and 'Cuopdansa.'
Migen means cup, lasan means board.Choice B can be ruled out because migen means cup.
Poen means walk; cuop means pull, and dansa means man. The only possible choice is C.
This option is incorrect.Cuop means pull

questions.explanation: Migen means cup; lasan means board; poen means walk; cuop means pull, and dansa means man. The only possible choices, then, are choices B and C. Choice B can be ruled out because migen means cup.

questions.text: Statements: Life means stresses and strains. Stresses and strains have an adverse effect on life.Conclusions:I. Life is not worth living.II. One should avoid stresses and strains in life.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I asserts that life is not worth living, which is a subjective assertion. The premises indicate that stresses and strains adversely affect life, but they do not imply that life itself is unworthy. Hence, this conclusion is not logically supported by the premises.

Conclusion II suggests that one should avoid stresses and strains in life. This follows logically from the premises, as they state that these factors have an adverse effect on life, implying that minimizing them would be beneficial.
This option is incorrect because Conclusion II does follow from the premises. The premises suggest that avoiding stresses and strains could improve life, thereby supporting Conclusion II.

options.text: Both conclusions I and II follow
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because while Conclusion II is logically supported by the premises, Conclusion I is not. The premises do not provide sufficient justification for the extreme assertion made in Conclusion I.

questions.explanation: The statements establish that stresses and strains are an inherent part of life and that they have a negative impact. It logically follows that avoiding these adverse effects would be beneficial, supporting Conclusion II. Conclusion I is an extreme, subjective judgment not supported by the statements.

questions.text: Statements: Company X has a record of manufacturing cameras of quality and the latest design so that you do not spoil even a single shot irrespective of the weather conditions.Conclusions:I. No other company except X is reputed in the camera industry.II. Anyone can take an acceptable shot with camera X.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The statement does not claim that no other company is reputable, it only highlights the strengths of Company X. Therefore, conclusion I is incorrect.

The statement suggests that the quality and design of Company X's cameras allow for good shots in any weather condition, implying that anyone can take an acceptable shot with their camera. Thus, conclusion II follows.
This option implies that one of the conclusions must be true, but since conclusion I is not valid, this option is incorrect.
Since conclusion II logically follows from the statement, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The statement claims the camera's quality prevents spoiling a shot in any weather, which implies that it is easy to use for anyone (Conclusion II). The statement makes no comparison to other companies, so Conclusion I, an absolute claim about other companies, does not follow.
